# edgekit

edgekit is a browser-native agent runtime with local-first, privacy-first defaults for adding AI workflows to existing web apps without making every interaction an unpredictable cloud-token cost. It uses browser-native models first, supports tuned model cascades and optional cloud fallback, and keeps app state, identity, tools, and approval boundaries under host control.

The project should be understood as open-source agent infrastructure, not a SaaS landing page. Developers usually arrive with a concrete blocker: unbounded token spend, sensitive app context, existing APIs that must remain authoritative, model-fit tradeoffs, slow orchestration, offline workflows, mutation approvals, auditability, RBAC-filtered tools, or safe MCP integration. The documentation maps those problems to Edgekit primitives.

## Documentation

- [Local-first agent sidecars](/docs/): edgekit adds agent workflows to existing web apps without forcing every prompt, tool call, and UI step through a metered cloud agent.
- [Quick start](/docs/getting-started/): Add the core package and web component, register tools, and mount the sidecar.
- [Core architecture](/docs/concepts/): Understand providers, fallback, tools, approvals, state, and the event stream.
- [API reference](/docs/api/): Typed runtime exports for providers, agents, memory, telemetry, audit, offline sync, and tool policy.
- [Enterprise controls](/docs/advanced/): Identity, RBAC, memory, audit, offline sync, tool policy, and observability primitives.
- [Ecosystem and integrations](/docs/ecosystem/): Framework wrappers, AG-UI backends, MCP tool catalogs, CRDT adapters, and future isolation adapters.
- [The edge-chat component](/docs/ui/): Use the Lit web component for the default sidecar UI, prompts, and approval controls.
- [Documentation index CLI](/docs/cli/): Build a portable docs index and expose it as an edgekit search tool.
- [Mission Profile authoring](/docs/mission-profiles/): Design Skills and Mission Profiles that localize one sidecar mission while executable tools remain app-owned.
- [Skill optimization](/docs/skill-optimization/): Use bounded, validation-gated Skill edits to improve agent outcomes without changing runtime model weights.
- [Production readiness](/docs/production/): Security, telemetry, local/cloud routing, approvals, and release checks for real deployments.
- [Testing outcome quality](/docs/outcome-quality/): Measure whether the agent achieved the workflow and answer quality the user needed.
- [Adopter simulation](/docs/adopter-simulation/): Time-boxed loops for proving both elite programmers and agent-assisted builders can reach production-grade sidecars.
- [Testing agent workflows](/docs/testing/): Use deterministic workflow tests for CI and real-model evals for provider quality.
- [Deployment and hosting](/docs/deployment/): GitHub Pages hosts the public docs. Cloudflare Pages or Vercel can provide WebLLM headers.
